export_view.value:
In RepairQ, your Catalog is a global list that applies to all locations, encompassing all your services and items. The Inventory Summary page is a comprehensive list of all Catalog items marked as "inventoried." You can view individually stocked inventory items, which vary in condition, status, cost, price, and other attributes. T When you have created a catalog item marked as "inventoried," it will appear on your Inventory Summary List with a quantity of -0-. There are various ways you can increase the quantity of these items:

  • Manual Inventory Adjustments - Adjust the quantity of individual items by navigating the Inventory Summary list. This process is demonstrated below.

  • Import Inventory Adjustments - Adjust bulk quantities of multiple items via our provided Import Template.

  • Add Inventory via Purchase Order - Adjust items into inventory by receiving them on a Purchase Order.

  • Add Inventory via Catalog Quick Create - Create new Catalog Items while working on tickets and add Instock quantities during the creation process.

Note: An item must be created in the Catalog before you can add/stock any inventory.

Additional Resources

  • Introduction to the Catalog - Start Here!

  • Creating a Catalog Item

  • Catalog Quick Create: Activation

  • Inventory Management: Adjusting Inventory Levels via Import

  • Purchase Order Resources

  • Inventory Status: What Does it Mean?

View the Inventory Summary List

You might find it helpful to think of this process as "stocking" inventory because you stock inventory items for each catalog item marked as "Inventoried."

From the top Navigation Bar, go to Inventory → Inventory Summary.

Manual Restock

Restock from the Inventory Summary Screen

When viewing the Inventory Summary list, click the Add Button (+) on the upper-right of the page.

Restock For a Specific Inventoried Item

When viewing the Inventory Summary list, use the filters to locate the specific item(s) you want to stock. Next to the line item, click the Options Button ( ⋮ ) to view available Actions. From the Action List, click Add (+).

Inventory Replenishment Form Fields

Add inventory by filling out the form details. At a minimum, you must add details for Item Type, Catalog Item Name, Supplier, Inventory Status, and Condition. Descriptions of important inventory attributes are listed below. When you save the form, the quantities are added to your inventory.

Note: Some fields may be pre-populated if added from a specific inventory item.

  • Item type - This is used to categorize your Catalog Items for Taxes, Cost of Goods, and other reporting.

  • Manufacturer - This is an additional level of categorization for Inventory Items.

  • Catalog Item - The name of the Catalog Item that you are replenishing.

  • Restock Quantity - The number of items you are currently adding to inventory.

  • Serial Numbers - This field is visible if the catalog item is "serialized.” You must enter a serial number for each inventory item added to your inventory. Enter one serial number per line.

  • Supplier - The supplying location/source of these items. This optional field is used for your records only.

  • Cost - This is the cost of the item from the Supplier. This will default to the value set in the Catalog Item details.

  • Price - This is the retail price of the item. This will default to the value set in the Catalog Item details.

  • Status - This is the Inventory Status that will be set when the item is moved into inventory. In most cases, the default status will be “Instock” for items that are ready to sell out on a ticket.

    • You may add inventory to other statuses. For example, you may add an inventory item as “Pending RMA” if you are returning a defective item to the manufacturer, or “Back Ordered” if you just purchased them from a supplier and want to enter them into the system as a placeholder.

    • For more information on inventory statuses, see our article, Inventory Status: What does it Mean?

After updating your inventory level, you are directed to the Inventory Detail page for that inventory item. This page lists all inventory records for that specific, inventoried catalog item. You can view details for current inventory status, date added into inventory, Price, Cost, Etc.

Note: Column data can be customized by updating your List Views.

Replenish via Import

A user with the Inventory Manager role or higher can make bulk adjustments to their inventory by importing data. This allows users to make inventory adjustments to Instock or Shrinkage status outside of an Inventory Count or by manual adjustment of individual items. If you discover items that need to be adjusted to Instock but are not on your spreadsheet, you can add new rows to the bottom of the template for the missing items. Once you enter the required column fields and enter the count, the Import Tool will adjust them into your inventory. For more information on this process, see our article, Inventory management: Adjusting Inventory Levels via Import.

Replenish via Purchase Order

Purchase Orders (POs) allow users to create a fulfillment record of the products they purchase from external suppliers. Working alongside RepairQ's reporting and inventory management features, you can create POs within the system to track spending on ordered products and parts and replenish onhand quantities for accurate inventory management. For more information on this process, see our article, Purchase Order Resources.

Stock a New Item With Catalog Quick Create

Catalog Quick Create allows users to create new catalog items directly from purchase orders and tickets. Creating a catalog item from a ticket will also allow you to update onhand quantities of inventory, so you can add and stock catalog items in real-time, without leaving the current ticket. For more information on this process, see our article, Catalog Quick Create: Activation.

There are a few things to remember for Quick Create items:

  • Items are created at the Global level, i.e. all locations will have access to this catalog item.

  • All roles that have access to creating tickets can quick-create catalog items.

  • Admins can choose which Item Types are used for Quick Create.

START 1.21.X
